edges.description: Ribosomal stress activates p53, leading to cell cycle arrest and apoptosis of erythroid progenitors.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing identifies GATA1 mutations resulting in Diamond-Blackfan anemia.
evidence.snippet: We performed exome sequencing on two siblings who had no known pathogenic mutations for DBA and identified a mutation in the gene encoding the hematopoietic transcription factor GATA1

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing identifies GATA1 mutations resulting in Diamond-Blackfan anemia.
evidence.snippet: the reduction in erythropoiesis associated with the disease can arise from causes other than defects in ribosomal protein genes

evidence.reference_title: Perspectives of current understanding and therapeutics of Diamond-Blackfan anemia.
evidence.snippet: Recent studies also indicated that non-RP genes like GATA1, TSR2, are associated with DBA

nodes.description: A distinct non-ribosomal mechanism causing DBA. GATA1 is an X-linked erythroid transcription factor essential for erythroid differentiation. Mutations in GATA1 (including splice site variants) reduce functional GATA1 protein levels, impairing erythroid gene expression. Unlike ribosomal protein mutations, GATA1 mutations directly affect erythroid transcription rather than global translation, but converge on the same erythroid differentiation failure.

biological_processes: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.snippet: the molecular diagnosis can be established in a male proband by identification of a hemizygous pathogenic variant in GATA1 or TSR2 associated with X-linked DBA syndrome

evidence.reference_title: Diamond-Blackfan anemia: genotype-phenotype correlations in Italian patients with RPL5 and RPL11 mutations.
evidence.snippet: About 20% of the patients screened had mutations in RPL5 or RPL11

evidence.reference_title: Diamond-Blackfan anemia: genotype-phenotype correlations in Italian patients with RPL5 and RPL11 mutations.
evidence.snippet: A close association was evident between RPL5 mutations and craniofacial malformations, and between hand malformations and RPL11 mutations

evidence.reference_title: Diamond-Blackfan anemia: genotype-phenotype correlations in Italian patients with RPL5 and RPL11 mutations.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in RPS24, RPS17, and RPL35A described in a minority of patients show that Diamond-Blackfan anemia is a disorder of ribosome biogenesis

evidence.reference_title: Diamond-Blackfan anemia: genotype-phenotype correlations in Italian patients with RPL5 and RPL11 mutations.
evidence.snippet: Two new genes (RPL5, RPL11), encoding for ribosomal proteins of the large subunit, have been reported to be involved in a considerable percentage of patients

evidence.reference_title: Diamond-Blackfan anemia: genotype-phenotype correlations in Italian patients with RPL5 and RPL11 mutations.
evidence.snippet: About 20% of the patients screened had mutations in RPL5 or RPL11

evidence.reference_title: The gene encoding ribosomal protein S19 is mutated in Diamond-Blackfan anaemia.
evidence.snippet: we identified mutations in RPS19 in 10 of 40 unrelated DBA patients
RPS19 | HGNC:10402 | Diamond-Blackfan anemia | MONDO:0015253 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Heterozygous loss-of-function mutations in ribosomal protein genes reduce ribosome assembly and protein synthesis capacity. Erythroid progenitors are exquisitely sensitive to ribosomal stress due to their high proliferative and translational demands. RPS19 mutations account for ~25% of cases, with RPL5, RPL11, and other RP genes collectively explaining ~65-70% of DBA.

evidence.reference_title: The gene encoding ribosomal protein S19 is mutated in Diamond-Blackfan anaemia.
evidence.snippet: we identified mutations in RPS19 in 10 of 40 unrelated DBA patients, including nonsense, frameshift, splice site and missense mutations

evidence.reference_title: The gene encoding ribosomal protein S19 is mutated in Diamond-Blackfan anaemia.
evidence.snippet: a function for RPS19 in erythropoiesis and embryogenesis

nodes.description: Ribosomal stress from RP haploinsufficiency leads to accumulation of free ribosomal proteins that bind MDM2, stabilizing p53. Activated p53 triggers cell cycle arrest and apoptosis preferentially in erythroid progenitors, resulting in selective red cell aplasia.

biological_processes: signal transduction by p53 class mediator

evidence.snippet: characterized by a profound normochromic and usually macrocytic anemia with normal leukocytes and platelets
